mnemonics: The 3 V's: Volume, Velocity, Variety - This trio defines the core challenge of Big Data.
keyPoints: Big data refers to extremely large datasets that are too complex for traditional data processing software.
It is characterized by the three V's: Volume (scale of data), Velocity (speed of data in/out), and Variety (range of data types and sources).
The primary goal of big data analytics is to uncover hidden patterns, correlations, and other insights to make better decisions.
commonMistakes: Thinking big data is only about the volume (size) of data and ignoring the importance of velocity and variety.
Assuming that traditional relational databases (like SQL) can efficiently handle all types of big data problems without specialized tools.
